mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signifies a robust labor market and strong consumer spending capacity, which underpins corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, low unemployment correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when UNRATE is below its 10-year average, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a critical threshold and continues to rise, it signals aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank rate hikes typically precede market downturns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances where FEDFUNDS meets the trigger condition, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with elevated consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong nominal consumer demand and pricing power for corporations, which can translate into higher revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns, even if real growth is moderate.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending is a key driver of economic growth.
failure_condition: If periods of high RSAFS growth and high CPIAUCSL growth consistently coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion reflect strong investment, consumer confidence, and future economic activity, providing a positive impulse to the S&P 500 over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength often precedes broader economic growth.
failure_condition: If HOUST values above their 5-year average consistently fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns during periods of economic expansion, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and increasing productive capacity, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output is a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are negative in more than 50% of months following a positive month-over-month change in INDPRO, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, reduced consumer spending, and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Sharp increases in unemployment often precede market declines.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a stagflationary environment, where economic growth is stagnant while prices rise, severely eroding corporate profitability and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary periods are historically detrimental to equity markets.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of periods identified as stagflationary by the trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) is a fundamental indicator of a healthy economy, driving corporate revenues and earnings, which in turn supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of equity market performance.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods following a quarter with GDPC1 growth above its 10-year average, this hypothesis is rejected.
